name: Vuelta al Embalse de Alloz desde Camping de Aritzaleku
region_intro: Mountain biking around Embalse de Alloz offers a variety of trails centered around the reservoir. The region features rolling hills, woodlands, and open landscapes, providing diverse terrain for mountain bikers. The area is characterized by its large body of water, which influences many of the circular routes. Trails often combine sections through forested areas with open views across the reservoir and surrounding countryside. Best mountain bike trails around Embalse de Alloz The most popular mountain bike trail is Vuelta al Embalse de Alloz desde Camping de Aritzaleku, a 13.5 miles (21.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 49 minutes to complete, offering views of the reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Embalse de Alloz – Camping Aritzaleku loop from Muez, a moderate 21.4 miles (34.4 km) path. This route features a mix of lakeside paths and sections through the surrounding hills. Local mountain bikers also love the Camping Aritzaleku – Castillo de Viguria loop from Allozko urtegia, a 14.1 miles (22.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain including woodlands and open fields, often completed in about 2 hours 4 minutes. Mountain biking around Embalse de Alloz is defined by reservoir-side paths, rolling hills, and scattered woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging routes. The routes in Embalse de Alloz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 80 reviews. More than 800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Embalse de Alloz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Embalse de Alloz?
A: There are over 100 mountain bike trails available around Embalse de Alloz, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has mapped 109 routes in total.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners around Embalse de Alloz?
A: Yes, Embalse de Alloz offers several trails suitable for beginners. While most routes are moderate, there are 8 easy routes that provide a gentle introduction to mountain biking in the area, often featuring smoother paths and less challenging ascents.

Q: What about more challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders will find 23 difficult mountain bike routes around Embalse de Alloz. These trails often feature significant elevation changes and more technical terrain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38203167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Alloz Reservoir – Embalse de Alloz loop from Allozko urtegia&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 42.1 km route with over 1000 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes around Embalse de Alloz?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Embalse de Alloz are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990044636/vuelta-al-embalse-de-alloz-desde-camping-de-aritzaleku" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta al Embalse de Alloz desde Camping de Aritzaleku&lt;/a&gt;, which circles the reservoir, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31886572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camping Aritzaleku – Castillo de Viguria loop from Allozko urtegia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Embalse de Alloz a good destination for family mountain biking?
A: Embalse de Alloz can be suitable for family mountain biking, especially on the easier and less technical sections. Look for routes with minimal elevation gain and well-maintained paths. The area's scenic beauty and reservoir views can make for an enjoyable family outing.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking around Embalse de Alloz?
A: The best time for mountain biking around Embalse de Alloz is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. The landscapes are also particularly vibrant during these times.

Q: Are there places to eat or get refreshments near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, you'll find options for refreshments, especially around the main access points of Embalse de Alloz, such as near Camping Aritzaleku and in the nearby villages. These often include cafes and restaurants where you can refuel after your ride.

Q: Where can I park when mountain biking at Embalse de Alloz?
A: Parking is generally available at various access points around Embalse de Alloz, including designated areas near Camping Aritzaleku and in the villages surrounding the reservoir. It's advisable to check specific starting points for routes for the most convenient parking options.

Q: Can I go wild swimming near the mountain bike trails?
A: Embalse de Alloz is a popular spot for water activities, including swimming. While not strictly 'wild swimming' in an unregulated sense, there are designated areas and beaches around the reservoir where you can cool off with a swim, often conveniently located near mountain bike trailheads.

Q: Are there camping options available for mountain bikers at Embalse de Alloz?
A: Yes, Camping Aritzaleku is a well-known campsite located directly on the shores of Embalse de Alloz, offering convenient accommodation for mountain bikers. It provides direct access to many trails and amenities for a comfortable stay.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails around Embalse de Alloz?
A: The mountain bike trails around Embalse de Alloz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 80 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the beautiful reservoir views, and the variety of routes suitable for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes in the Embalse de Alloz area?
A: For those looking for longer rides, Embalse de Alloz offers several extended routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38192968" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Alloz – Church of San Salvador loop from Allozko urtegia&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 43.9 km route, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38203167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Alloz Reservoir – Embalse de Alloz loop from Allozko urtegia&lt;/a&gt; covers 42.1 km with significant climbing.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the mountain bike trails around Embalse de Alloz?
A: Mountain biking around Embalse de Alloz offers diverse scenery, including expansive views of the reservoir, sections through rolling hills, and shaded paths through scattered woodlands. You'll experience a mix of open landscapes and natural forest environments, often with the shimmering water as a backdrop.
tips: Beautiful panoramic views from the road towards the reservoir and against the Alloz reservoir and the valley of the Salado river
